growth_records.strain: uncultured natural population (not isolated; identified via amoA/rrs genes)
growth_records.medium: Natural Antarctic continental shelf seawater (West Antarctic Peninsula, AASW/WW/CDW water masses); 96 h dark shipboard incubation of whole seawater with 15N-tracer

up.units: d-1 equivalent AO-rate-derived; not a per-cell uptake rate

growth_records.cite: Tolar et al. Contribution of ammonia oxidation to chemoautotrophy in Antarctic coastal waters. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.61. [PMC5113851]
growth_records.snippet: population growth rates averaged 0.50 per day (all amoA data, range 0.01-2.5 per day) or 0.31 per day (all rrs data, range 0.01 to 2.1 per day).
growth_records.notes: Not a lab culture: field/in-situ estimate for an uncultured natural archaeal population. Paper's calculation chain: (1) ammonia-oxidation (AO) rate measured by 15NH4Cl tracer incubation (96 h, 0C, dark); (2) chemoautotrophic C fixation = AO rate / 10 (10 mol NH4 oxidized per mol C fixed, cited from Berg et al. 2015); (3) new Thaumarchaeota cell production = AO rate x (46.6-52.4 cells produced per nmol ammonium oxidized, mean 49.5, from Konneke et al. 2005 and Martens-Habbena et al. 2009); (4) population growth rate = new-cell-production-rate / standing Thaumarchaeota abundance (from qPCR of amoA or rrs gene copies). We converted the paper's reported per-day growth rate (mean 0.50/d using amoA-based abundance; range 0.01-2.5/d) to per-hour by dividing by 24 (0.50/24=0.0208 h-1; range 0.01/24-2.5/24 = 0.00042-0.104 h-1). Values are averages across all seasons, depths and water masses combined (AASW/WW/CDW). No defined growth medium exists (natural seawater); organism not culturable/strain-resolved. Growth-rate range: 0.01-2.5 per day (amoA-based).

growth_records.strain: uncultured natural population (not isolated; identified via amoA/rrs genes)
growth_records.medium: Natural Antarctic continental shelf seawater (West Antarctic Peninsula, AASW/WW/CDW water masses); 96 h dark shipboard incubation of whole seawater with 15N-tracer
growth_records.cite: Tolar et al. Contribution of ammonia oxidation to chemoautotrophy in Antarctic coastal waters. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.61. [PMC5113851]
growth_records.snippet: population growth rates averaged 0.50 per day (all amoA data, range 0.01-2.5 per day) or 0.31 per day (all rrs data, range 0.01 to 2.1 per day).
growth_records.notes: Same calculation chain as the amoA-based record in this file, but here Thaumarchaeota standing abundance was estimated from rrs (16S rRNA) gene qPCR instead of amoA. Paper-reported mean = 0.31 per day (range 0.01-2.1 per day), converted to per hour by dividing by 24: 0.31/24=0.0129 h-1; range 0.01/24-2.1/24 = 0.00042-0.0875 h-1. Same caveats as above record (uncultured natural population, no defined medium, averaged across seasons/water masses).

growth_records.strain: uncultured natural population (not isolated; identified via amoA gene)
growth_records.medium: Natural Antarctic continental shelf seawater, late-winter surface mixed layer (AASW) vs. summer samples, all depths pooled
growth_records.cite: Tolar et al. Contribution of ammonia oxidation to chemoautotrophy in Antarctic coastal waters. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.61. [PMC5113851]
growth_records.snippet: with higher growth rates during late winter than summer (all depths: amoA means 1.1 versus 0.26 per day, P<0.0001; rrs means 0.45 versus 0.25 per day, P=0.092).
growth_records.notes: Season-resolved subset of the same amoA-based population-growth-rate calculation described above (AO-rate-derived cell production / amoA-based Thaumarchaeota abundance). Late winter mean = 1.1 per day = 1.1/24 = 0.0458 h-1 (this record). A companion summer value (0.26 per day = 0.0108 h-1) is reported in the paper as significantly lower (P<0.0001) but is not separately entered here to avoid over-fragmenting near-duplicate rows; see source_snippet for both numbers verbatim.
